stem: The characteristic polynomial of \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] & [[c]] \\ [[f]] & [[e]] & [[g]] \\ [[h]] & [[k]] & [[j]] \end{bmatrix} \) is \( p(\lambda) = \lambda^{3} - c_{2}\lambda^{2} + c_{1}\lambda - c_{0} \). What is the value of \( c_{2} \)?
solution: The coefficient \( c_{2} \) of the characteristic polynomial equals the sum of the eigenvalues, which is the trace of \( A \): \( [[a]] + [[e]] + [[j]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies the diagonal entries; \( c_{2} \) is their sum (the trace), not their product.
This adds only two of the three diagonal entries.
This adds only two of the three diagonal entries.
This adds only two of the three diagonal entries.
This is one more than the correct trace.
This is one less than the correct trace.
This is three more than the correct trace.

stem: The characteristic polynomial of \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] & [[c]] \\ [[f]] & [[e]] & [[g]] \\ [[h]] & [[k]] & [[j]] \end{bmatrix} \) is \( p(\lambda) = \lambda^{3} - c_{2}\lambda^{2} + c_{1}\lambda - c_{0} \), where \( c_{0} = \det(A) \). What is the value of \( c_{0} \)?
solution: The constant term \( c_{0} \) of the characteristic polynomial equals \( \det(A) \). Expanding the determinant gives \( c_{0} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( c_{2} \) (the trace), not \( c_{0} \) (the determinant).
This multiplies only the diagonal entries, ignoring the off-diagonal contributions to the determinant.
This has the right idea (product of diagonal entries) but the wrong sign and still ignores off-diagonal terms.
This computes only one 2x2 cofactor, not the full 3x3 determinant.
This is one more than the correct determinant.
This is two less than the correct determinant.
This is four more than the correct determinant.

stem: The characteristic polynomial of \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] & [[c]] \\ [[f]] & [[e]] & [[g]] \\ [[h]] & [[k]] & [[j]] \end{bmatrix} \) is \( p(\lambda) = \lambda^{3} - c_{2}\lambda^{2} + c_{1}\lambda - c_{0} \), where \( c_{1} \) is the sum of the three \( 2 \times 2 \) principal minors of \( A \) (the minors obtained by deleting one row and the matching column). What is \( c_{1} \)?
solution: The three principal \( 2\times2 \) minors are \( [[m1]] \) (deleting row/col 1), \( [[m2]] \) (deleting row/col 2), and \( [[m3]] \) (deleting row/col 3). Their sum is \( c_{1} = [[m1]] + [[m2]] + [[m3]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only one of the three principal minors instead of summing all three.
This multiplies the minors instead of adding them.
This subtracts the middle minor instead of adding all three.
This is \( c_{2} \) (the trace), not \( c_{1} \).
This is one more than the correct sum of minors.
This is two less than the correct sum of minors.
This is three more than the correct sum of minors.

stem: Let \( A = \begin{bmatrix} [[a]] & [[b]] & [[c]] \\ [[f]] & [[e]] & [[g]] \\ [[h]] & [[k]] & [[j]] \end{bmatrix} \) and let \( p(\lambda) = \det(\lambda I - A) \) be its characteristic polynomial. What is \( p([[t]]) \)?
solution: \( p([[t]]) = \det\!\big([[t]]I - A\big) \), the determinant of \( [[t]]I - A \), which evaluates to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: \( p(t) \) is only \( 0 \) when \( t \) happens to be an eigenvalue; that is not guaranteed here.
This keeps only the leading term \( t^{3} \) and drops the lower-order contributions from \( A \).
This treats \( p(t) \) as linear in \( t \) instead of evaluating the full cubic determinant.
This drops the \( c_{1}t - c_{0} \) terms of the characteristic polynomial.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.
This is five more than the correct value.

stem: A \( 3 \times 3 \) matrix \( A \) has eigenvalues \( [[r1]] \), \( [[r2]] \), and \( [[r3]] \), so its characteristic polynomial is \( p(\lambda) = (\lambda - [[r1]])(\lambda - [[r2]])(\lambda - [[r3]]) = \lambda^{3} - c_{2}\lambda^{2} + c_{1}\lambda - c_{0} \). What is \( c_{1} \)?
solution: By Vieta's formulas, \( c_{1} \) is the sum of the pairwise products of the eigenvalues: \( [[r1]][[r2]] + [[r1]][[r3]] + [[r2]][[r3]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( c_{2} \), the sum of the eigenvalues, not the sum of their pairwise products.
This is \( c_{0} \), the product of all three eigenvalues, not the sum of pairwise products.
This uses only one pairwise product instead of summing all three.
This adds a leftover eigenvalue to a single pairwise product instead of summing all three products.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is two less than the correct value.
This is four more than the correct value.
